title: callicarpa subintegra
intro: Callicarpa subintegra, also known as the purple callicarpa, is a plant species in the Lamiaceae family that is native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Callicarpa subintegra can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
